page: Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g is a general-purpose virtual server belonging to the VX1 Cloud Compute family. It features 8 vCPUs powered by the AMD EPYC-Turin processor architecture (x86_64) running at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The CPU allocation is shared, utilizing 8 physical cores with 1.0 thread per core. The system is equipped with 32.0 GB of system memory, yielding a balanced 4.0 GB of RAM per core. Storage is network-based, configured with a minimal 1 GB allocation, and the instance includes one complimentary public IPv4 address. No dedicated GPU hardware is present.

Performance benchmarks indicate strong single-core capabilities alongside average multi-core and memory throughput. In single-threaded tasks, the server performs in the top tier (top 10%), as demonstrated by stress-ng single-core CPU performance and single-threaded gzip compression. However, multi-core execution falls into the average category, with stress-ng multi-core and PassMark CPU scores aligning with the middle 50% of tested instances. Memory bandwidth is similarly average, reaching 72.12 GB/sec for large block reads and 41.41 GB/sec for large block writes. For lightweight machine learning workloads, the server achieves a strong text generation speed of 324.23 tokens/sec on a small 135M LLM, though prompt processing for larger 7B models is weak, registering at 27.21 tokens/sec.

From a resource density perspective, the vx1-g-8c-32g offers a cost-efficient profile for workloads requiring substantial memory and high single-thread performance without the premium of dedicated CPU cores. The primary tradeoff is the shared CPU allocation, which can introduce performance variability under sustained multi-core loads, and the highly restricted 1 GB of network storage, which necessitates external storage attachments for data-heavy applications. This configuration is best suited for general-purpose workloads, lightweight web serving, Redis caching, and single-threaded data processing tasks.
description: Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g is a general-purpose virtual server from the VX1 Cloud Compute family, featuring 8 shared vCPUs on the AMD EPYC-Turin x86_64 architecture running at 2.0 GHz. It is configured with 32.0 GB of RAM, 1 GB of network-based local storage, and no GPU. Benchmarks show top-tier single-core CPU performance and single-threaded compression speeds, while multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations sit in the average tier. The shared CPU allocation and minimal local storage represent key tradeoffs, making this instance a cost-efficient option for workloads that do not require sustained multi-core execution or large local disk space. It is highly suited for general-purpose applications, static web serving, Redis caching, and lightweight text generation tasks.
og_description: Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g is a general-purpose server featuring 8 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and 1 GB network storage, delivering top-tier single-core performance and average multi-core efficiency.
meta_description: Vultr vx1-g-8c-32g is a general-purpose server with 8 shared AMD EPYC vCPUs, 32 GB RAM, and top-tier single-core performance.
tagline: A balanced shared-core virtual server combining top-tier single-threaded performance with a high memory-to-vCPU ratio for general-purpose workloads.
bullet_points: 8 shared AMD EPYC-Turin vCPUs on x86_64 architecture
32.0 GB of system memory providing 4.0 GB per core
Top-tier single-core CPU and single-threaded compression performance
1 GB network-based local storage and one public IPv4 address
Best suited for general-purpose applications, web serving, and caching
